I basically just said, “Hey guys, come on. I don’t have time to write all of
these direct mail packages and so I want you guys to help me.”
Not a one of them had written a long copy magalog, not one of them. I
showed them how to write them. I led them through. I copy-chiefed them.
Then I really relied on them heavily.
Now I owned the client’s backend, or at least part of it, not the whole
backend, a part of it back then and I made money a couple of ways. I took
half of the advance, and I took half of the royalty. None of these writers were
at a level where they could earn $50, $1,000 and then $25,000 advance.
They were all making just about half that, so it was a win-win.
I paid them the regular fees. I was able to accept new customer acquisition
promotions that otherwise I would have had to decline or just never would
have gotten done, and so that was free money to me basically.
Then they came in and created these promotions. Some of them were just
phenomenally successful. Bob Hutchinson and I created the “Super Net”
package which mailed for almost three years, and all of them did great work.
I had an override on the backend, so every time an existing customer bought
something, I got a piece of that so I was making money again because they
were growing our file. As the file grew larger, our sales to existing customers
grew larger, so that worked great.
More recently I had a situation where I didn’t want to handle the frontend at
all. I just wanted to focus on the backend because that’s where the money
was, but we needed to grow the frontend.
So I had the client call another friend of mine Eric Betuel and Eric’s like

-- 36 of 38 --

Makepeace Business System
Module 1 • Introduction
Transcript 	Page 3 of 38
The Total Package 	www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
one of the best copywriters Rodale ever had. He’s written huge controls for
Phillips and many other top mailers.
I brought Eric in and Eric wrote the frontend and created thousands of new
customers, and then I made a bunch of money selling them stuff. Today I’ve
got four full-time copywriters on staff and we’re following the Agora model
here.
Each one of them gets a good basic income, and that’s to keep body and
soul together but not to get rich on, and then each one receives 10% of the
money that they make me. On just one client, each one of them has the
potential to make high 6 figures or even a million dollars a year in royalties.
I decided to go that direction. I was ready for it, actually establishing an
agency. You don’t have to go that way. You can allow that stuff to be done
by other freelancers and take a commission or a piece of the action for
introducing them to the client.
Tony: 	Yeah, that makes sense. I’m starting to get that.
Cool. Thanks for doing this, Clayton.
It was a good call and you did answer a lot of my questions.
Clayton: 	Yeah, it really helps me think my way through it because a lot of this I’ve
just kind of done instinctively and intuitively over the years. The fact that it’s
worked out like gangbusters for us and for our clients is fortuitous, but I wish
I could say, “28 years ago, I sat down and I came up with this great new way
that’s so much better,” but I really just fell into it.
The stuff I’ll be sharing is more real lessons from actually doing it, and I
want to paint the picture for you of what it feels like to have a client who’s
so in love with you that he refuses to write checks. He insists on wiring your
royalties into your account directly on the 15th of every month.
When you get your bank statement, you open it up and look at it, “Oh, we
got $650,000 this month. Oh, we got $300,000 this month.” That’s not an
exaggeration.
I was actually there once when this one client told his accountant to send
the money and he was giggling on the phone, “Yeah, send Clayton a half a
million dollars this month. That’s his royalty.” He was laughing because he
made ten times that.

-- 37 of 38 --

Makepeace Business System
Module 1 • Introduction
Transcript 	Page 38 of 38
The Total Package 	www.makepeacetotalpackage.com
Tony: 	That’s great, Clayton. Thanks a lot. I appreciate it. I hope everybody got a lot
out of it.
Clayton: 	Cool. Thank you, Tony.
Tony: 	All right. Take it easy.
Clayton: 	Take care.
This ends Module 1 …
Talk about some great stuff!
But don’t stop yet, because we’re just getting started.
We’ve barely scratched the surface of The Makepeace Business System, and there’s
plenty more still to come.
In the next module, Clayton’s going to really dive into the details … and reveal just how
you can profit from his partnership model, whether you’re a copywriter, consultant, or
business owner.
So what are we waiting for?
Grab Module 2, slap it in your DVD player, and get ready to take plenty of notes.
Because if you like what you’ve learned so far, just wait until you see this next one!

-- 38 of 38 --
